100 Years Ago Today, This is What Was Happening ...

On March 23, 1926, the League of Nations convened in Geneva, working to address global disarmament amidst rising tensions in Europe. In the United States, President Calvin Coolidge faced mounting pressure to bolster economic recovery efforts as the nation continued to navigate the post-war landscape. Meanwhile, in Egypt, political unrest surged as nationalists demanded greater autonomy from British rule, highlighting the growing push for independence in various colonies.
